title: Post-IPO Liquidity Stress Syndrome
text:
Post-IPO Liquidity Stress Syndrome

Will Denyer

Gavekal Research | 12 Jun 2026

SpaceX just raised a record US$75bn in its initial public offering on Thursday and may raise another US$11bn through the exercise of the greenshoe, or overallotment, option. As investors make room in their portfolios for a deal that could ultimately total US$86bn, this is temporarily weighing on demand for other equities. However, given the massive scale of the US equity market and the cash returned to investors through dividends and buybacks, the market should be able to digest an IPO of this size with little more than a temporary period of volatility.The real medium-term risks are that (i) this is only the start of a broader capital-raising trend, which looks increasingly likely by the day, and (ii) Nasdaq’s rule changes to include SpaceX at an accelerated pace and at a multiple of free float could damage its reputation as a reliable marketplace. That, at least, was the argument made last week (see Mega-IPOs And Reputation Risk). However, this is not the whole story. As an astute client pointed out, the real problem may come when up to half of SpaceX’s total market capitalization is unlocked two to six months after the IPO.
